promote.rationale: Apex advisory executive organ under Basic Law Arts. 54-56; indispensable counterpart to Chief Executive and Administrative Secretary already in core.
Statutory body (Cap. 93) for civil-service appointments, promotions and discipline; fills the public-service employment and discipline gap alongside Civil Service Regulations.
Central doctrinal category for administrative adjudication and tribunal decision-making; anchors the natural-justice / procedural-fairness cluster together with audi alteram partem and Wednesbury.
HK-specific term of art for delegated legislation made by statutory bodies (e.g. MTR Cap. 556 Bylaws, urban council bylaws); fills the sources-of-delegated-legislation gap.
Trigger concept for public-law grievance mechanisms (Ombudsman, regulator complaint-handling); complements Ombudsman already in core and opens the public-law-complaints sub-area.

curation_principles: The five promotions are selected to close identifiable gaps in the existing 45-term administrative core while preserving conceptual centrality to Hong Kong administrative law as it is actually taught and practised. The starting set is strong on the remedial side of judicial review (certiorari, mandamus, prohibition, judicial review, Wednesbury) and on regulatory instruments (Ordinance, regulation, licence, permit, Gazette), but it lacks anchors in three doctrinal regions that any standard treatise (Wesley-Smith, Chen, or Wan & Chan) would treat as foundational. Executive Council and Public Service Commission together introduce the institutional architecture of HK executive government (Basic Law Arts. 54-56) and of public-service employment and discipline (Cap. 93), complementing Chief Executive-adjacent terms already in core. Quasi-judicial supplies the doctrinal category that underwrites natural-justice requirements for tribunals and decision-makers, articulating what audi alteram partem and Nemo judex actually attach to. Bylaw is the HK-specific nomenclature for delegated legislation made under statutory authority, opening the sources-of-subordinate-legislation sub-area that was otherwise represented only by the generic Ordinance and regulation. Complaint extends the Ombudsman anchor into the broader public-law-complaints machinery (statutory complaint mechanisms under Cap. 397 and sectoral regulators). Each promoted term has a clean zh_canonical from the HK DOJ Glossary, non-trivial e-Legislation attestation, and is not a near-synonym of any existing core entry: together they convert the current core from a list of remedies and instruments into a balanced map of HK administrative law.
